types.name: Water
types.effects: Non-massive, non-flying ground units pushed or moved into water drown instantly. Massive units (all Mechs, boss Vek) survive but are submerged — cannot attack or repair. Flying units hover over water with no effect. Extinguishes fire on units that enter.
types.notes: Mechs ARE Massive and CAN voluntarily enter water tiles, but become submerged and cannot attack. Push non-massive Vek into water for instant kills. Freezing water creates Ice tiles.

types.name: Mountain
types.effects: Impassable obstacle. Blocks movement and projectiles. Has 2 HP — any attack damages it. First hit creates Damaged Mountain; second hit destroys it into Mountain Rubble (passable ground).
types.notes: Takes 2 hits to destroy regardless of damage per hit. Instant-kill attacks destroy even undamaged mountains in one hit. Mountains can be shielded. Cataclysm squad specializes in destroying them.

types.name: Building
types.effects: Civilian structures tied to Grid Power. Damage to buildings reduces Grid Power. Buildings have a Grid Defense % chance to resist damage.
types.notes: Protecting buildings is the primary objective. Each building lost reduces Grid Power. At 0 Grid Power, game over. Buildings are immune to fire damage. Buildings block both movement and projectiles for ground and flying units.

types.name: Forest
types.effects: When the tile itself takes damage (not collision/push damage to a unit on it), it catches fire and becomes a Forest Fire tile. Forest Fire sets any unit ending its turn on it on fire.
types.notes: Can be strategically set on fire to create persistent damage zones. Good for Flame Behemoths. Freezing a Forest Fire tile reverts it to a regular Forest.

types.name: Sand
types.effects: When hit by weapon damage, converts to Smoke tile. Push damage and fire damage do NOT convert sand to smoke. If hit by a fire attack, converts to Fire Tile instead of Smoke.
types.notes: Found on R.S.T. island. Only weapon damage triggers the smoke conversion. Lightning environmental effect also converts sand to smoke.

types.name: Ice
types.effects: When damaged, cracks. Second hit destroys, creating Water tile. Fire effects skip the damaged stage and instantly convert Ice to Water.
types.notes: Found on Pinnacle island. Two-step destruction: intact -> cracked -> water. Freezing damaged ice restores it. Freezing water creates ice.

types.name: Lava
types.traversable: Depends on unit type (behaves like water)
types.effects: Behaves like water for drowning purposes. Non-massive, non-flying ground units die instantly. Massive units (Mechs, boss Vek) survive but are submerged AND set on fire. Flying units hover safely over lava.
types.notes: Appears on Volcanic Hive (final mission). NOT simply 'traversable with fire damage' — it drowns non-massive ground units just like water. Push non-massive enemies into lava for instant kills.

types.name: Chasm
types.effects: Any non-flying unit pushed into a chasm dies instantly, INCLUDING massive units and boss Vek. Unlike water/lava, Massive trait does not help. Frozen flying units pushed into chasms also die.
types.notes: Instant kill that bypasses Massive trait. Found on R.S.T. island and created by Cataclysm squad and Seismic Activity environmental effect.

types.name: Conveyor Belt
types.effects: Pushes any unit (including flying units) one tile in the belt's direction during the environment step of the Attack Order phase.
types.notes: Found on Detritus island. Can push mechs, Vek, and objects. Plan positioning around belt direction. Push direction is fixed and marked on the tile.

types.name: A.C.I.D. Pool
types.effects: Units stopping on A.C.I.D. pool tiles gain A.C.I.D. status (weapon damage doubled, armor disabled). Consumed after one use.
types.notes: Found on Detritus island. Push enemies into pools to apply A.C.I.D., then hit them for double weapon damage. Flying units ARE affected by A.C.I.D. Pools (unlike water/lava).

types.name: Teleporter Pad
types.effects: Teleporter pads come in two pairs (4 pads total — blue pair and red pair). Units voluntarily ending movement on a pad OR pushed onto a pad are teleported to the paired pad. If the destination pad is occupied, the two units swap positions.
types.notes: Found on Pinnacle island. Push also triggers teleportation, not just voluntary movement. Can teleport Vek to unexpected positions for creative kills.

types.name: Fire Tile
types.effects: Units entering or ending turn on fire tiles are set on fire. Persists until extinguished by smoke or freezing.
types.notes: Created by fire attacks, burning forests, and some environmental effects. Fire replaces smoke when applied to a smoke tile.

types.name: Smoke Tile
types.effects: Units in smoke cannot attack or repair. Enemy units that END their turn on a Smoke tile have their queued attack cancelled for the entire turn.
types.notes: Key mechanic for Rusting Hulks. The attack cancellation applies to enemies that end their turn on smoke — not just units that pass through. Smoke extinguishes fire on tiles and units.

types.name: Cracked Ground
types.effects: Weakened ground tile. If damaged again, shatters into a Chasm, destroying any non-flying unit on it.
types.notes: This is NOT the same as Vek spawn tiles. Cracked Ground is a terrain feature (introduced/expanded in Advanced Edition via Cataclysm mechanic) that becomes a Chasm on damage. Spawn tiles are separate indicators showing where Vek will emerge.

types.name: Spawn Tile
types.effects: Telegraphed indicator showing where a Vek will emerge at end of turn. Can be blocked by placing any unit on the tile, which deals 1 damage to the blocker but prevents the Vek from emerging.
types.notes: Separate from Cracked Ground terrain. Spawn tiles appear at the end of the enemy turn showing next turn's spawns. Blocked Vek attempt to spawn again next turn. Stabilizers passive nullifies blocking damage to mechs.

status_effects.name: Fire
status_effects.effect: Unit takes 1 damage at the START of each turn (during Attack Order processing, before enemy attacks). Persists until removed.
status_effects.applied_by: Fire attacks, fire tiles, burning forests, lava (for surviving massive/mech units)
status_effects.removed_by: Repairing, entering water (submerging extinguishes fire), entering smoke, being frozen
interactions: Applying fire to a frozen unit unfreezes the unit AND catches it on fire simultaneously
Shield blocks fire application but does not remove existing fire
Fire melts ice tiles instantly (skips damaged stage, goes straight to water)
Trains, buildings, and Renfield Bombs are fire-immune
Burrowers lose fire when they burrow underground
A.C.I.D. does NOT double fire damage (fire is not weapon damage)
Setting a unit on fire also sets the tile on fire

status_effects.name: Frozen
status_effects.effect: Unit cannot move, attack, or take actions. Any damage to a frozen unit is negated but unfreezes it. Frozen units on water create ice tiles instead of drowning.
status_effects.applied_by: Cryo-Launcher, ice attacks, Freeze Mines, Ice Storm environmental effect
status_effects.removed_by: Taking any damage (damage is negated), repairing (uses action to break free), fire damage (unfreezes AND catches fire)
interactions: Frozen flying units are grounded — can fall into water/chasms and die
Frozen enemies cannot attack (cancels their telegraphed attack)
Frozen units on spawn points block emergence
Ice Mech freezes itself when using Cryo-Launcher (unless shielded)
Shield prevents freezing
Frozen units can still be pushed by weapons
Frozen units are NOT protected from instant-kill effects (Self-Destruct, chasms, environmental kills)
Casting shield on a frozen unit makes it both frozen AND shielded (resistant to 2 attacks)

status_effects.name: Shield
status_effects.effect: Blocks the next instance of damage AND prevents application of negative status effects (Fire, Frozen, A.C.I.D., Web). Consumed on first hit.
status_effects.applied_by: Defense Mech shield, Bethany Jones ability, Mafan (Zoltan) ability, Adam pilot ability, some upgrades
status_effects.removed_by: Taking direct damage (shield absorbs the hit and breaks)
interactions: Shields do NOT remove existing status effects — only prevent new ones
Shields prevent Fire damage from fire tiles
Shields prevent A.C.I.D. application (without consuming the shield)
Shields prevent Freezing
Shields on buildings protect them from one attack
Multiple shields do not stack — a unit either has shield or doesn't
CANNOT block instant-kill effects: Self-Destruct, Satellite Rocket, Supply Train ramming, Chasm death, Mosquito Leader attack, Air Bombing, Lightning Strikes
Freeze Mine: shielded unit removes mine without being frozen and without losing shield

status_effects.name: Smoke
status_effects.effect: Units in smoke cannot attack or repair. Enemy units that END their turn on a Smoke tile have their queued attack cancelled for the entire turn.
status_effects.applied_by: Smoke attacks, sand tiles hit by weapon damage (not push/fire damage), Storm Generator, various weapons
status_effects.removed_by: Smoke dissipates after the turn it was applied (lasts 1 full turn cycle). Fire replaces smoke.
interactions: Attack cancellation triggers for enemies that end their turn on smoke
Smoke extinguishes fire tiles when applied
Smoke extinguishes burning units that enter smoke tiles
Storm Generator (Rusting Hulks passive) deals 1 damage to all enemies in smoke
Camila Vera is immune to smoke effects
Smoke prevents satellite launches on Pinnacle
Smoke does NOT prevent: explosions, Vek spawns, death-effects (like Explosive Decay)

status_effects.name: A.C.I.D.
status_effects.effect: Persistent status that doubles WEAPON damage against this unit. All other damage (push, collision, fire, spawn blocking, electric smoke) is NOT doubled. Disables Armor.
status_effects.applied_by: Centipede attacks, A.C.I.D. pool tiles, A.C.I.D. Tile (for surviving massive/mech units), A.C.I.D. weapons
status_effects.removed_by: Repairing (active repair action only — Technician passive and Blood Psion healing do NOT remove it)
interactions: Only doubles weapon damage — push, collision, fire, spawn blocking damage are NOT affected
A.C.I.D. DOES apply to self-damage from weapons like Unstable Cannon
A.C.I.D. DOES double the bonus damage from a Boosted attacker
Completely disables Armor while active
Kills of A.C.I.D.-affected units leave acid pools on ground tiles
Flying units are affected by A.C.I.D. Pool tiles (unlike water/lava)
Shield prevents A.C.I.D. application without consuming the shield
Essential for 'Overkill' achievement (8 damage to single unit)

status_effects.name: Webbed
status_effects.effect: Unit cannot move but CAN still attack and use abilities.
status_effects.applied_by: Spider and Alpha Spider attacks, Spiderling web attack, Scorpion attacks
status_effects.removed_by: Being pushed to an empty tile (even if captor also moves in same direction), dashing away, captor's attack being cancelled, captor dying, repairing
interactions: Camila Vera is immune to webbing
Shield prevents web application
Webbed mechs can still attack and use weapons — only movement is restricted
Webbed Vek can still attack from current position
Teleporter and similar weapons do NOT remove Webbed status
If a webbed mech uses Teleporter/Swap, the swapped unit becomes webbed instead

status_effects.name: Boosted
status_effects.effect: Unit's next attack deals +1 damage. Consumed on any ability use (including non-damaging abilities and repair). Boosted repair heals 2 HP instead of 1.
status_effects.applied_by: Raging Psion (all Vek boosted while alive), Morgan Lejeune kills, Kai Miller full HP, stepping on fire (Heat Sinkers), some weapons
interactions: Affects ALL targets of an attack, including self-damage
Consumed even by non-damaging abilities like shields or teleports
Boosted repair heals 2 instead of 1
A.C.I.D. doubles the Boosted bonus damage (stacks multiplicatively)
Deployable and spawned units (Light Tank, Arachnoid) are NOT affected by Boost
Can be very dangerous on Vek with area attacks

status_effects.name: Armored
status_effects.effect: Reduces incoming WEAPON damage by 1. Does NOT reduce push damage, collision damage, fire damage, spawn blocking damage, or electric smoke damage. Does NOT reduce self-damage.
status_effects.applied_by: Abe Isamu ability, Shell Psion buff (all Vek), Hook Mech natural armor, Judo Mech natural armor, Bouncer Leader natural armor, Psionic Receiver passive
status_effects.removed_by: A.C.I.D. (disables armor entirely while active). Shell Psion death removes Psion-granted armor.
interactions: Only reduces WEAPON damage — push, collision, fire, spawn blocking, electric smoke are all unaffected
Does NOT reduce self-damage from weapons (Armor only applies to damage from OTHER sources' weapons)
Minimum 1 damage still applies — armor doesn't negate 1-damage weapon attacks
A.C.I.D. completely disables armor
Multiple instances of Armored on the same unit are redundant (do not stack)
Also reduces Psion Tyrant's Hive Targeted damage

basic_rules: Damage is applied immediately when an attack resolves
Player attacks resolve in a specific order displayed in the Attack Order indicator (NOT simultaneously)
After player ends turn, effects resolve in Attack Order: fire damage, healing, environmental effects, enemy attacks, spawns
Push/pull happens simultaneously with the attack's damage
Units pushed into walls or other units take 1 bump damage
Units pushed into water/lava die (non-massive, non-flying) or are submerged (massive). Units pushed into chasms die (all non-flying).

push_mechanics.description: Many attacks push targets. Push interactions are critical.
rules: Push moves target 1 tile in the specified direction
If target cannot move (wall, mountain, edge of map, another unit, building), target takes 1 bump damage instead
If two units collide from a push, BOTH take 1 bump damage. Pushing into a building also deals 1 bump damage to the building (costs grid power). (Empirically verified in v1.2.93.)
Pushing into water kills non-massive non-flying ground units instantly; massive units submerge
Pushing into lava: same as water, but surviving massive units are also set on fire
Pushing into chasms kills ALL non-flying units including massive
Pushing into fire tiles sets unit on fire
Pushing onto conveyor belts: conveyor effect applies during environment step of Attack Order
Pushing frozen flying units into water/chasms kills them (frozen = grounded)
Pushing onto teleporter pads triggers teleportation

grid_power.description: Grid Power represents the overall power grid of the civilian buildings. At 0, game over.
grid_power.losing_power: Each building destroyed reduces Grid Power by the number of buildings on that tile (1-3)

grid_defense.overpower_bonus: Each Grid Power gained when already at max: +2% for the first 5 points, then +1% per point, up to +25% total from overpower.
grid_defense.pilot_bonus: +3% per pilot with '+3 Grid DEF' skill. Max 3 pilots = +9%.
grid_defense.maximum_total: 49% (15% base + 25% overpower + 9% pilot). 34% on Unfair (0% base + 25% overpower + 9% pilot).
grid_defense.seeded_rng: Resist outcomes are predetermined by seeded RNG. Reset Turn does NOT change the outcome.

gaining_power: Some missions grant Grid Power as a bonus objective reward
After finishing a Corporate Island, spend 1 Reputation for 1 Grid Power
Perfect Island reward: can choose +2 Grid Power
Grid Charger weapon: restores 1 Grid Power when used successfully
On Unfair difficulty, all grid health earned from the above sources is doubled
grid_power.game_over: If Grid Power reaches 0, the timeline is lost. One surviving pilot can be sent back to try again in a different timeline.

reactor_cores.description: Used for upgrading Mechs. Each installed core gives 1 energy for stats, weapons, upgrades, passives, or pilot abilities.
sources: Recovering a Time Pod during a mission
Protecting a key building during a mission (bonus objective)
Spending 3 Corporate Reputation Points after protecting a Corporate H.Q.

phases.name: Player Planning Phase
phases.description: View all enemy telegraphed attacks. Move each mech once and use one weapon per mech. A mech cannot move after attacking (move first, then attack). Order matters — some actions create openings for others.

phases.name: Attack Order Resolution
phases.description: After player ends turn, all effects resolve in a specific Attack Order (viewable in-game by hovering 'Attack Order' or holding Alt). The order is: 1) Fire damage, 2) Healing effects, 3) Environmental effects and enemy attacks interleaved in displayed order, 4) Vek spawns, 5) Psion passive effects.

attack_order_details: 1. Fire damage (burning units take 1 damage — units at 1 HP die before they can act)
2. Healing effects (Technician pilot, Blood Psion regeneration)

3. Environmental effects (air strikes, high tide, lava flow, lightning, seismic activity, volcanic projectiles, falling rocks, tentacles, ice storms, conveyor belts) — interleaved with enemy attacks in a specific displayed order
4. Enemy Vek attacks execute (in the order displayed in the Attack Order indicator)
5. Vek spawn (emerge from spawn tiles — blocked by any unit on the tile)
6. Surviving Vek and newly emerged Vek reposition and telegraph next turn's attacks
important_notes: Fire damage happens BEFORE enemy attacks — can kill a burning 1HP Vek before it attacks
Environmental effects (air strikes, lightning, etc.) happen as part of the interleaved Attack Order, typically before most enemy attacks
Conveyor belts push during the environment step of the Attack Order phase
Smoke cancels attacks for enemies that END their turn on smoke
Blocked spawns damage but don't kill the Vek — it will try again next turn
The player always acts first with perfect information about all telegraphed attacks

easy.alpha_vek: No alpha Vek outside of 3-objective missions on the first and second islands
easy.vek_spawns: Fewer Vek spawn across all missions
easy.rare_vek: Islands cannot have more than one type of rare Vek
easy.notes: Recommended for learning. All achievements except 'Hard Victory' can be earned on Easy.

normal.alpha_vek: Alpha Vek appear outside of 3-objective missions starting from the second island
normal.rare_vek: Fourth island has two types of rare Vek
normal.notes: Default difficulty. Good balance of challenge and achievement hunting.

hard.alpha_vek: Alpha Vek appear more often, can be encountered on any mission starting from the first island
hard.rare_vek: Third and fourth islands have two types of rare Vek
hard.volcanic_hive: A Boss Vek appears on the first phase of the Volcanic Hive
hard.notes: Required for 'Hard Victory' achievement. Significantly harder.

unfair.fourth_island_bosses: A Vek Leader appears in every regular mission on the 4th island, replacing one possible enemy with a Boss variant (except Spider Leader and island Psion). HQ mission on 4th island has two bosses.
unfair.volcanic_hive: Two bosses in each phase during 3 or 4 island playthroughs
unfair.notes: Advanced Edition difficulty. Extremely challenging. No specific achievements require Unfair.

advanced_edition_mechanics.bosses: AE adds Boss Vek encounters at the end of each island. Bosses have unique mechanics and high HP.
advanced_edition_mechanics.unfair_difficulty: New maximum difficulty level. See difficulty section for full details.

new_psions.smoldering_psion: Grants fire immunity to all Vek (ongoing while Psion alive)
new_psions.arachnid_psion: When any Vek dies, a Spiderling Egg is spawned on the tile where it died (ongoing while Psion alive). NOT the same as Blast Psion — does NOT grant Explosive Decay.
new_psions.raging_psion: All other Vek are Boosted (+1 damage to attacks), ongoing while Psion is alive

advanced_edition_mechanics.boost_mechanic: New status effect. +1 damage on next ability use. Central to Heat Sinkers squad.
advanced_edition_mechanics.new_squads: Added 5 new squads: Bombermechs, Arachnophiles, Mist Eaters, Heat Sinkers, Cataclysm.
advanced_edition_mechanics.new_pilots: Added 4 new pilots: Kai Miller, Rosie Rivets, Morgan Lejeune, Adam.
advanced_edition_mechanics.new_weapons: 39 new weapons and equipment, including Grid Charger (restores Grid Power).
